scenarios.title: Database Connection Pool Exhaustion
scenarios.incident_summary: Intermittent 503 errors on order-related endpoints. Latency spikes observed across multiple services. Database team has not reported any issues.

ground_truth.root_cause: PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion on order-db caused by a connection leak in order-svc after a recent deployment. Connections are opened but never returned to the pool, causing cascading failures.

error_patterns: FATAL: remaining connection slots are reserved for superuser
Connection pool exhausted — max_connections=100 all in use
Unable to acquire connection within 30s timeout
pg_stat_activity shows 98 active connections (limit: 100)
warn_patterns: Connection pool utilization at 95% — approaching limit
Slow query detected: 8200ms for SELECT on orders table
Lock contention detected on table: order_items

error_patterns: Database connection timeout after 30000ms
Failed to execute query — no available connections
Transaction rolled back — connection pool timeout
Upstream order-svc returned 503 Service Unavailable
warn_patterns: Retry budget exhausted for order-svc — returning 503
Unusual spike in authentication requests — possible retry storm from clients

alert_config.title: Database Connection Pool Near Capacity
alert_config.description: PostgreSQL connections at 98/100. New connections are being rejected.

alert_config.title: High Error Rate on Order Service
alert_config.description: Error rate exceeded 20% for order-svc. Multiple database connection timeouts observed.

alert_config.title: Elevated 503 Responses
alert_config.description: 503 response rate on /api/orders at 12%. Upstream order-svc returning errors.

scenarios.title: Bad Deployment Causes Latency Cascade
scenarios.incident_summary: Significant latency increase across order processing pipeline. P99 latency jumped from 200ms to 8000ms in the last 30 minutes. No recent infrastructure changes reported but a deployment was rolled out to order-svc earlier today.

ground_truth.root_cause: A recent deployment to order-svc introduced an N+1 query bug in the order listing endpoint. Each order list request now fires 200+ individual database queries instead of a single batch query, causing order-db CPU saturation and cascading latency to api-gw.

error_patterns: Request GET /api/orders timed out after 30000ms
Thread pool exhausted — all 200 worker threads busy
Slow database query: 4200ms for SELECT on orders JOIN order_items
Too many connections — approaching max_connections limit
Query execution time 5200ms exceeds statement_timeout=30s threshold for monitoring
warn_patterns: Slow query log: 200+ identical SELECT statements from single session

error_patterns: Upstream order-svc response timeout after 30s
504 Gateway Timeout for GET /api/orders

warn_patterns: Cache miss rate elevated: 45% (baseline: 12%) — possible cache key change after deployment

alert_config.title: P99 Latency SLA Breach
alert_config.description: Order service P99 latency at 8200ms, exceeding 500ms SLA. Started after deployment v2.14.0 at 09:00 UTC.

alert_config.title: Database CPU Saturation
alert_config.description: order-db CPU utilization at 92%. Unusually high query volume detected.

alert_config.title: Gateway Timeout Errors Increasing
alert_config.description: 504 error rate on order endpoints at 18%. Upstream order-svc not responding within timeout.

scenarios.title: Cache Eviction Storm
scenarios.incident_summary: Sudden spike in response times across all customer-facing endpoints. The cache service memory usage is fluctuating wildly. Multiple services reporting increased latency to downstream dependencies.

ground_truth.root_cause: A runaway batch job is flooding the cache with large temporary keys, triggering aggressive LRU evictions of hot production data. This causes a thundering herd of cache misses, overwhelming the order-db and payment-svc with requests that would normally be served from cache.

error_patterns: OOM prevention: evicting 15000 keys to free memory (used: 3.8GB/4GB)
Eviction rate: 5200 keys/sec — 10x above normal baseline
WARNING: memory fragmentation ratio 2.1 — allocation failures possible
Large key detected: batch:export:chunk_4582 (size: 48MB) — exceeds max-key-size recommendation
warn_patterns: Keyspace notifications: 12000 key eviction events in last 60s

error_patterns: Cache MISS for hot key order:catalog:pricing — falling back to database
Database query surge: 500 requests/s (normal: 50)
warn_patterns: Response latency 3500ms — cache fallback to DB

error_patterns: Cache MISS for payment:config:rates — loading from cold storage
Payment processing delayed: currency rate lookup timed out
warn_patterns: Exchange rate cache expired — 3 sequential misses
Rate limiting activated for /api/orders — backpressure applied
Query volume spike: 500 qps (baseline: 50) — possible cache miss storm
CPU utilization 82% — elevated due to increased query load

alert_config.title: Cache Memory Pressure Critical
alert_config.description: Redis memory at 3.8GB/4GB. Aggressive LRU eviction in progress. Hit rate dropped to 22%.

alert_config.title: Cache Miss Rate Elevated
alert_config.description: Cache miss rate for order-svc at 78%. Database fallback queries increasing.

alert_config.title: Database Query Volume Spike
alert_config.description: Query volume increased 10x from baseline. Likely cache miss storm.

alert_config.title: Payment Processing Delays
alert_config.description: Payment confirmation latency increased. Cache misses on exchange rate data.
